6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"86e90e723ff64b82a67d967c664420620256aae5","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:04:50.205555594Z","description":"PROBLEM
`ddx-0793ac75` is stale in the broad-federation sense. The live read-federation coverage already proves the hub/spoke read path in `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation_test.go` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts`, so this bead now only needs to track the remaining owner-targeted write-routing gap.

ROOT CAUSE
`TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-350` already splits the work into TC-017 worker control and TC-018 bead writes. The read side is covered by `TestFederation_QueryShape_TwoHealthySpokes`, `TestFederation_PartialResult_OneSpokeOffline`, and `TestFederation_VersionSkew_RendersAndSkips`, which leaves only the owner-write routing pieces as durable follow-up work. The stale dependency edge to cancelled `ddx-387a0178` no longer belongs on the live queue.

PROPOSED FIX
- Narrow this bead so it names only the missing owner-write coverage.
- Preserve the FEAT-026 / Story 14 references.
- Keep the current read coverage cited explicitly so the queue does not treat the read path as pending work.
- Remove the blocking dependency on cancelled `ddx-387a0178` unless a note proves it is still load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement new federation code.
- Do not change the live read-federation behavior.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Do not rewrite execution-audit commits.

`ddx-28f3cf37` still reads like a broad federation UI follow-up, but the real gap is the owner-targeted worker-control path. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338` keeps TC-017 planned, while `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177` already defines the write-routing contract and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214` already spells out the expected start/stop/offline/idempotency behavior at the UI level.

ROOT CAUSE
The current bead is still coupled to the broader federation UI story in `ddx-28f3cf37`, but the concrete contract is already isolated in FEAT-026 and the dedicated worker-control spec. What is missing is a narrower bead body that only tracks the live owner-targeted write-routing implementation and the Go-side tests that prove `startWorker` and `stopWorker` reach exactly one owning spoke.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-28f3cf37` so it covers only worker start/stop owner-targeted writes, not read-federation smoke.
- Name the missing implementation tests explicitly as `TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Keep the bead aligned to FEAT-026 and TC-017 instead of duplicating the read-only federation tests.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new `/federation` route read coverage.
- Do not touch bead/document writes here.
- Do not add ts-net or cross-host networking beyond the local loopback gate.
- Do not modify the FEAT-026 text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-359e812b` so it narrows to live owner-targeted bead create/edit and documentWrite persistence.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate child if it is still load-bearing.
- Add or update the missing owner-write tests as `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not change the mocked Playwright smoke paths unless they are being split out of this bead.

If Playwright is used, `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or the new sibling e2e file passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-07-03T04:45:37.248329614Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-20e7ebf0","depends_on_id":"ddx-3f3e1cbe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-07-03T04:45:37Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Forwarded federation writes need an autonomous idempotency proof. Retrying the same hub-issued command with the same request id must replay the original result and must not launch duplicate workers, append duplicate bead events, or write the same document twice.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:338 defines TC-017.5 idempotency for federated worker commands.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:379 defines TC-020.6 idempotency for mutating managed-node commands.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarded commands to carry request id, target node/project, and expected version metadata.
- Existing GraphQL idempotency tests such as cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:101-154 and resolver_operator_prompt_test.go:218-231 cover local idempotency surfaces, but the federation owner-write path needs its own forwarded-command coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add focused Go tests or live multi-process tests for forwarded federation write idempotency.
- Use the same request id/idempotency key for two identical hub-issued worker, bead, or document write commands.
- Assert the second response returns the original result/deduplicated state and does not duplicate spoke-local state.
- Cover at least two command classes: worker start and one persistent data mutation (`beadCreate` or `documentWrite`).
- Keep the test deterministic with loopback servers and synthetic request ids; do not rely on wall-clock sleeps beyond short polling for process readiness.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not build a new global idempotency framework unless existing request-id plumbing cannot support the tests.
- Do not add real network/tailnet tests.
- Do not change unrelated local run/operator-prompt idempotency behavior.

ROOT CAUSE
FEAT-026 routes `beadCreate`, `beadUpdate`, `beadApprove`, `beadBlock`, `beadCancel`, `beadReopen`, and `documentWrite` only to the owner node and never broadcasts (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`). The stale bead mixed that live owner-targeted write persistence work with already-covered UI smoke and the separate ts-net guard.

PROPOSED FIX
- Track only the live owner-targeted write persistence gap: bead create/edit and `documentWrite` forwarding, offline-spoke refusal, and idempotent request IDs.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate bead if it still needs work.
- Guard the narrowed scope with `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Do not expand this bead back into federation read smoke or mocked Playwright smoke coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths into this bead.

In-scope files:
- `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts`

Out-of-scope (separate bead):
- ts-net guard work if it remains open
- managed-node outbound control from ADR-028

2. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_BeadMutationRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued bead create/edit for a spoke project mutates the spoke project and not the hub project.
3. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_DocumentWriteRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves hub-issued `documentWrite` for a spoke `docs/helix/**` path writes only under the spoke root and preserves/returns forwarding metadata where available.
4. At least one live write path includes an offline-spoke refusal assertion with no phantom hub-local row.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/... ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... -run 'Federation|FanOut|RouteMutation|ForwardMutation|Spoke' -count=1` passes.\n6. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` passes, or the new sibling e2e file named in this bead passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-07-03T04:45:36.880769405Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3f3e1cbe","depends_on_id":"ddx-b303cf96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-07-03T04:45:36Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The federation suite proves hub/spoke registration, federated reads, offline badges, restart recovery, and mocked UI mutation states, but it does not yet prove the most important live owner-targeted write contract: commands issued from a hub for a spoke-owned project must execute on the spoke and must not create hub-local phantom state.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:34-39 says FEAT-026/ADR-007 require owner-targeted federation writes and that hub/spoke writes must never broadcast.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarding for `startWorker`, `stopWorker`, bead mutations, and `documentWrite`, with the hub never creating local fallback state for a spoke write.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:335-337 requires spoke worker start/stop forwarding and offline refusal.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:350 and :363 require federated bead and document writes.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307 currently exercises live two-node reads/offline/restart but not live owner-targeted write persistence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the live multi-process federation coverage, preferably in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or a sibling e2e file, to start one hub and one spoke with isolated temp roots and per-node XDG state.
- From the hub, issue a worker start/stop for a spoke project and assert the worker exists in the spoke project worker registry, not the hub registry.
- From the hub, create or edit a bead in the spoke project and assert the spoke `.ddx/beads.jsonl` changes while the hub project does not receive a phantom local bead.
- From the hub, write a `docs/helix/**` document in the spoke project and assert the file changes on the spoke root only and origin/forwarding metadata is observable where the API exposes it.
- Include an offline-spoke refusal assertion for at least one write path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add real ts-net/MagicDNS coverage; loopback hub/spoke is the autonomous local gate.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028; this bead covers FEAT-026 inbound spoke owner writes only.

- Remove the blocking dependency on cancelled `ddx-387a0178` unless a note proves it is still load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE

5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... -run 'Federation|Hermetic' -count=1` passes if Go-side helper code is added.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-07-03T04:45:37.628669226Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b51238ca","depends_on_id":"ddx-3f3e1cbe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-07-03T04:45:37Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The live multi-node federation e2e tests must be safe to run autonomously on a developer machine or worker host. They should not leak server processes, fixed ports, XDG state, project registration, or git dirt into the DDx checkout. Without an explicit guard, future federation e2e additions can silently depend on developer state or leave artifacts that block `ddx work` pre-claim checks.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:104-125 already shows the correct pattern for persisted spoke identity and per-node `XDG_DATA_HOME`, but there is no explicit guard that the suite leaves the repository state unchanged.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:116-118 starts hub/spoke processes using `--hub-mode` and `--hub-address`; new tests must keep using random loopback ports and cleanup.
- Recent queue operation was blocked by staged `.ddx/executions/**` and `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` runtime artifacts, proving autonomous tests/workers need stronger cleanup assertions before they can be trusted in continuous queue drain.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a hermeticity guard around the live federation e2e harness or a dedicated test that snapshots git state before/after the federation suite.
- Assert hub/spoke tests use temp project roots, temp `XDG_DATA_HOME`, temp `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, and dynamically allocated loopback ports.
- Ensure all spawned hub/spoke processes are terminated on failure and no `.ddx/workers`, `.ddx/executions`, or project registration artifacts are written under the repository root except pre-existing dirt.
- If the guard cannot run inside Playwright reliably, add a small script/test wrapper that runs the e2e file and verifies `git status --short` is unchanged modulo pre-existing paths captured before the run.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not require a clean worktree at test start; compare before/after so the guard tolerates pre-existing operator dirt.
- Do not add visual screenshot checks.
- Do not run real ts-net/MagicDNS in this autonomous gate.

Hub-mode already has a generic owner-targeted mutation transport, but documentWrite still follows the local filesystem write path. A spoke-owned project edited from the hub would therefore persist on the hub filesystem instead of the owning spoke.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 writes document content locally after path resolution.
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go:81-198 already implements ForwardMutation with origin identity, forwarding path, target node/project, expected version, and typed refusal handling.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:7-9 documents that the federation provider surface already includes owner-targeted write forwarding.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without a routing-aware input.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect hub-mode spoke-owned document targets in the resolver and route them through Federation.ForwardMutation instead of local os.WriteFile.
- Carry origin identity, forwarding path, request id, idempotency key, target node/project IDs, and expected version through the forward request and returned response.
- Preserve local path confinement for same-node writes and for non-hub requests.
- Surface typed refusal errors for offline, stale, read-only, missing-owner, or broadcast-like targets instead of silently falling back to local state.

NON-SCOPE
- Stale-content conflict checking and graph rebuild behavior are owned by sibling bead ddx-3c399bec.
- Playwright spec-editing coverage stays with ddx-e951255c.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql_mutation_docs_test.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider_test.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_hub_test.go

When a spoke node starts with --hub-address, it must register with the hub and maintain a heartbeat so the hub's FederationRegistry knows the spoke is alive. Today no --hub-address flag exists and no spoke lifecycle (register / heartbeat / stale detection / URL-change re-register) is implemented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/server.go: no --hub-address flag wired.
- cli/internal/federation/: no spoke-side registration client or heartbeat loop exists.
- The hub's FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012, a dep) provides the register endpoint and stale detection, but the spoke side has no code to call it.
- Per locked decisions from the federation planning: register is idempotent on stable node_id; duplicate node_id rejected (not silently merged); heartbeat jittered at 30s; stale threshold at 2 minutes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add --hub-address string flag to the server command (cli/cmd/server.go or equivalent).
- Add cli/internal/federation/spoke_lifecycle.go:
  - RegisterWithHub(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID, localURL): idempotent POST to hub register endpoint.
  - StartHeartbeat(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID): jittered 30s heartbeat loop; on failure → stale detection.
  - HandleURLChange(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID, newURL): re-register when local URL flips.
- Stale threshold: hub marks spoke offline after 2 minutes without heartbeat.

NON-SCOPE
- Hub-side registry implementation (ddx-90f48012, a dep).
- Federation query fan-out (existing fanout.go).
- Mutation routing (S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d).","events_attachment":"ddx-b69f04f8/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-b69f04f8","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:14","area:server","kind:feature"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

Hub-mode already has a generic owner-targeted mutation transport, but documentWrite still follows the local filesystem write path. A spoke-owned project edited from the hub would therefore persist on the hub filesystem instead of the owning spoke.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 writes document content locally after path resolution.
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go:81-198 already implements ForwardMutation with origin identity, forwarding path, target node/project, expected version, and typed refusal handling.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:7-9 documents that the federation provider surface already includes owner-targeted write forwarding.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without a routing-aware input.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect hub-mode spoke-owned document targets in the resolver and route them through Federation.ForwardMutation instead of local os.WriteFile.
- Carry origin identity, forwarding path, request id, idempotency key, target node/project IDs, and expected version through the forward request and returned response.
- Preserve local path confinement for same-node writes and for non-hub requests.
- Surface typed refusal errors for offline, stale, read-only, missing-owner, or broadcast-like targets instead of silently falling back to local state.

NON-SCOPE
- Stale-content conflict checking and graph rebuild behavior are owned by sibling bead ddx-3c399bec.
- Playwright spec-editing coverage stays with ddx-e951255c.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql_mutation_docs_test.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider_test.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_hub_test.go

Out-of-scope (file a separate bead):
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts

Hub-mode document saves for spoke-owned projects have no owner-targeted documentWrite forwarding path. After local documentWrite gains an expectedHash optimistic-concurrency contract, the same mutation also needs to forward spoke-owned project writes to the owning spoke instead of creating or overwriting a hub-local file.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34 defines DocumentWrite as a local path/content mutation with no project-owner forwarding semantics.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:55 writes content directly with os.WriteFile, so hub-mode requests are not routed to the owning project.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3263 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without forwarding metadata; sibling bead ddx-3c399bec defines the local expectedHash/version contract this bead must preserve across federation.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation_workers.go and cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go contain the existing project identity / owner routing surface used by hub-to-spoke operations; documentWrite must use the same registered project owner source rather than inferring ownership from local paths.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:356 requires spec/document edits to be project-scoped, path-confined, and owner-targeted in hub-mode.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:353 defines TC-019 federated document write coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Build on ddx-3c399bec: documentWrite accepts path, content, and expectedHash; this bead must not invent a second version token.
- For hub-mode requests targeting a spoke-owned registered project, resolve the project owner from the same project identity / federation registry used by existing hub-to-spoke worker operations, then forward documentWrite to that owner.
- Include forwarding origin metadata such as originServerID and forwarded=true on the internal request so the spoke can reject recursive forwarding loops and execute the write locally.
- Preserve path confinement on the spoke using ResolveDocumentPath and preserve expectedHash conflict semantics from ddx-3c399bec; stale expectedHash must return a deterministic conflict without writing content.
- Ensure successful forwarded writes persist on the owning spoke and do not create or overwrite a hub-local file for the spoke project.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full collaborative editor or arbitrary filesystem browser. Keep the workflow scoped to registered project documents, especially docs/helix/**. Do not redesign the federation protocol beyond the minimum documentWrite forwarding metadata. Do not implement frontend Playwright coverage in this bead. Title:    federation: owner-targeted bead/document writes
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 3
Labels:   phase:2,  area:federation,  area:web,  area:server,  area:tests,  kind:test,  story:14,  spec:FEAT-026,  ac-quality:needs-refinement
Parent:   ddx-a038a090
Desc:     PROBLEM
`ddx-359e812b` is broader than the remaining gap. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` already isolates TC-018 and TC-019 for bead and spec/document writes, while the existing Playwright coverage in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256` already covers the mocked forwarded paths. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
FEAT-026 routes `beadCreate`, `beadUpdate`, `beadApprove`, `beadBlock`, `beadCancel`, `beadReopen`, and `documentWrite` only to the owner node and never broadcasts (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`). The stale bead mixed that live owner-targeted write persistence work with already-covered UI smoke and the separate ts-net guard.

PROPOSED FIX
- Narrow this bead to live owner-targeted bead create/edit and `documentWrite` persistence.
- Keep offline-spoke refusal and request-id idempotency as explicit acceptance points.
- Track the ts-net guard as a separate bead if it still needs work.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths into this bead.
Accept:   1. `ddx bead show ddx-359e812b` reads as a live owner-targeted bead/document write bead, or the bead is closed/superseded.
2. The bead body names `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.
3. The reconciliation notes cite `TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363`, `beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268`, `spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, and `federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` passes, and `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.

    <title>federation: narrow the bead/document write follow-up bead</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx-359e812b` is still too broad for what remains. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` keeps bead, spec, and document writes planned, but the current Playwright suite already covers the mocked forwarded paths in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, while `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
The stale bead conflates three concerns: hub/spoke federation reads, mocked UI write-forwarding, and the live owner-targeted persistence gap. FEAT-026 says forwarding is owner-targeted and never broadcast (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`), so the bead should only track the missing live bead/document write persistence and refusal behavior, not the already-covered smoke paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-359e812b` so it narrows to live owner-targeted bead create/edit and documentWrite persistence.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate child if it is still load-bearing.
- Add or update the missing owner-write tests as `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not change the mocked Playwright smoke paths unless they are being split out of this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-a038a090`. Upstream stale bead context: `ddx-28f3cf37`.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `ddx bead show ddx-359e812b` is narrowed to live owner-targeted write persistence or is closed/superseded.
2. The bead body names `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.
3. The reconciliation notes cite `TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363`, `beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268`, `spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, and `federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` passes, and `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>

`ddx-359e812b` is still too broad for what remains. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` keeps bead, spec, and document writes planned, but the current Playwright suite already covers the mocked forwarded paths in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, while `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
The stale bead conflates three concerns: hub/spoke federation reads, mocked UI write-forwarding, and the live owner-targeted persistence gap. FEAT-026 says forwarding is owner-targeted and never broadcast (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`), so the bead should only track the missing live bead/document write persistence and refusal behavior, not the already-covered smoke paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-359e812b` so it narrows to live owner-targeted bead create/edit and documentWrite persistence.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate child if it is still load-bearing.
- Add or update the missing owner-write tests as `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not change the mocked Playwright smoke paths unless they are being split out of this bead.

Body covers the core stance: favor real e2e tests (no mocks at e2e layer); stubs over mocks at unit layer; contract tests between stub and real backend; zero mocks in integration tests; behavior over implementation; deterministic factories; behavior-naming; testing pyramid as shape not ratios; performance measurements require baseline + explicit boundary + reproducible harness. grep -E \"cli/internal/|docs/helix/|orchTestGitOps|fakeExecuteBeadGit|gateTestGitOps|mockExecutor|script\\.go|integration_helper_test\" library/personas/test-engineer.md returns no matches. No hard numeric coverage threshold presented as a universal rule. No \"TDD is the only valid approach\" framing. No retry-loop-around-flaky-test as acceptable pattern. No performance claim pattern without baseline + boundary. Sources section cites at least two external works.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).\n\nRename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.\n\n**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**\n- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.\n- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.\n- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.\n- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.\n- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.\n- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.\n- Test names describe behavior (\"creates invoice when all line items valid\"), not positions (\"test_create_invoice_3\").\n- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.\n- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is \"fast\", \"scales\", or \"improved performance\" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. \"It feels fast\" and \"benchmarks look good\" are not performance claims.\n\n**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**\n- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (\"\u003e= 80% overall\", \"100% on critical paths\"). Replace with: \"measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target\".\n- Specific timing budgets as universal rules (\"unit \u003c 10ms\", \"integration \u003c 100ms\"). Replace with: \"tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern\".\n- Specific pyramid percentages (\"60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e\") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.\n- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD \"worst practice\".\n- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).\n- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.\n- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.\n- Retry loops around flaky tests as \"improvement\" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.\n- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say \"coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs\".\n\n**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**\n- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic \"a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary\")\n- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base\n- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md\n- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent\n\nGround in external canon:\n- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example\n- Martin Fowler - \"Mocks Arent Stubs\" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)\n- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)\n- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: test-engineer\nroles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]\ndescription: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement\ntags: [testing, quality, performance]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); helix-test-engineer plugin persona (HELIX concern)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54.294846139Z","id":"ddx-13ff86c6","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Generalize test-engineer persona — strip DDx-internal path references","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:39:32.555980009Z"}

The existing file is a textbook summary of C4, ADR, microservices, CQRS, SAGA, event sourcing — a laundry list of patterns without opinionated voice. Rewrite with real stances.\n\nCore stances:\n- Simplicity beats sophistication — the right architecture is the simplest one that works for the real load, not the one that scales to 10x imagined load\n- Prefer monolith first — microservices have operational cost that only pays back at real scale\n- Event-driven only when async decoupling earns it — not by default\n- Data model beats clever caching — design the schema right, the cache falls out naturally\n- Write ADRs for decisions that will bite future maintainers; don't write ADRs for decisions with no real alternatives\n- Most 'architecture' failures are requirements failures or team-structure failures in disguise\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nAnti-patterns:\n- Designing for 10x scale before validating 1x\n- Microservices because 'teams should own services' without validated team boundaries\n- Event sourcing for audit because 'it's clean' — audit logs solve audit without event sourcing's operational tax\n- Abstracting over unclear requirements (YAGNI at the architecture layer)\n- Reaching for a pattern name before understanding the actual constraint\n\nSources:\n- Anthropic Prompt Library architecture prompts (if any)\n- Gregor Hohpe's architecture writings\n- Stefan Tilkov's monolith/microservices talks\n- Martin Fowler 'Monolith First' and similar\n- Reference to the C4 model (Simon Brown) and ADR (Michael Nygard) as conventions for output, not dogma\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: architect\nroles: [architect, technical-lead]\ndescription: Opinionated systems architect — simplicity-first, monolith-first, decisions with ADRs when they bite maintainers\ntags: [architecture, design, simplicity]\n---\n\nIn-scope:\n- library/personas/architect.md (new)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- Deleting architect-systems.md (deprecation bead)\n- Other persona rewrites (separate beads)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:41:46.498027045Z","id":"ddx-2bc4d02c","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Rewrite architect persona with real opinions on when to reach for each pattern","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:45:00.829035311Z"}

Sources section cites at least two external works. grep -E \"cli/internal/|docs/helix/\" library/personas/implementer.md returns no matches.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:41:46Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1513225","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:54.294309117Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-2da7da89","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"Replace library/personas/pragmatic-implementer.md with library/personas/implementer.md. The existing file has a generic tutorial tone; rewrite with real opinions.\n\nCore stances:\n- YAGNI — implement only what is specified; no speculative features or \"while we are here\" additions\n- KISS — simplest solution that meets requirements; avoid clever code and premature optimization\n- DOWITYTD (Do Only What I Told You To Do) — stop when the task is complete\n- Ship tests with code — implementations without a test command referenced in AC are half-done (but: test-first vs test-alongside vs test-after is a workflow choice, not mandated by this persona — see the test-engineer persona for the testing stance; this persona requires tests exist and pass, not a specific methodology)\n- Do not design for hypothetical future requirements. Three similar lines is better than a premature abstraction.\n- **Performance claims require baselines.** Do not add code \"for performance\" without a baseline measurement and a clear boundary describing what is being measured. \"This is faster\" without a number is not a justification. (Cross-reference test-engineer for the measurement discipline.)\n- **Favor real e2e validation at the integration boundary.** When in doubt between a layer of abstraction/mocking and writing a real integration test, write the real integration test. The cost of mocks compounds; the cost of a real e2e test is paid once.\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nAnti-patterns to call out:\n- Speculative abstraction (base classes, interfaces, config knobs for \"someday\")\n- Refactoring unrelated code during a bug fix (\"while we are here...\")\n- Adding error handling for scenarios that cannot happen (trust internal invariants; validate at system boundaries)\n- Leaving TODO comments for problems that should block the current change\n- Comments that restate what the code does (well-named identifiers already do that)\n- Adding a mock or stub when a real integration test would be simpler and catch more bugs\n- Making performance claims without baselines or boundaries\n\nSources:\n- YAGNI principle (c2.com wiki, various Martin Fowler articles)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library entries for coding agents, if any fit\n- Relevant DDx system-prompt content from library/prompts/claude/system-prompts/focused.md (already documents YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD)\n- Brendan Gregg on performance measurement discipline (baseline + boundary)\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: implementer\nroles: [implementer, software-engineer]\ndescription: Ships the specified scope, nothing more — YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD with tests included, real e2e favored over mocks, performance claims only with baselines\ntags: [implementation, shipping, minimalism]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/implementer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: Deleting pragmatic-implementer.md (deprecation bead); other persona rewrites (separate beads)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:41:46.437282625Z","id":"ddx-2da7da89","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Rewrite implementer persona with opinionated YAGNI/KISS stance","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:45:00.750092403Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:373:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer opening the document graph, I want a structured integrity panel that groups real document problems by kind and points me at the files to fix, so I can triage a noisy graph quickly and confirm when it is healthy.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` (or a sibling `Issues` field) is a typed slice with `Kind`, `Path`, `ID`, `Message`, and optional `RelatedPath`. Kinds include at minimum: `duplicate_id`, `parse_error`, `missing_dep`, `id_path_missing`, `id_path_mismatch`, `required_root_missing`, `cascade_unknown`.\n2. GraphQL schema exposes `DocGraph.issues: [GraphIssue!]!` with the same fields. The existing string `warnings` either stays (rendered from issues) or is deprecated with a schema comment.\n3. Unit tests in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` cover each issue kind: fixtures that produce exactly one instance of that kind and assert the typed output.\n4. `/graph` page renders an **Integrity** panel when `issues.length \u003e 0`:\n   - grouped by `kind` with a count per group;\n   - each row shows path, id, and message;\n   - path links to the document viewer (reuses existing documents route);\n   - total-issues badge appears next to the page title.\n5. With zero issues, neither the panel nor the badge renders, and the graph view is visually identical to a healthy graph today.\n6. Duplicate-ID rows show both paths and a \"copy suggested unique ID\" button; the suggestion is deterministic (same input → same output) and a unit test asserts the suggestion function's stability.\n7. `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`) CLI command prints the same issue list grouped by kind, exits 0 if empty and 1 if any issues present. Integration test asserts exit codes and grouped output.\n8. Playwright e2e:\n   - navigates to `/graph` on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target;\n   - asserts the Integrity panel shows groups \"Duplicate ID (1)\" and \"Missing dep target (1)\";\n   - expands the Duplicate ID group and asserts both paths are visible;\n   - clicks the first path and asserts navigation to the documents route for that file;\n   - on a clean fixture repo, asserts neither the panel nor the badge is present.\n\n**Dependency:** best sequenced after `ddx-12cae4dd` lands so the fixture repos are not polluted by `.claude/worktrees` noise, but implementation does not block on it — the two can proceed in parallel and this bead's tests use synthetic fixtures.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-23T04:15:59Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3704155","closing_commit_sha":"8b291ee475105211ea3b3369e7bf0e88c82166a8","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:09:46.473146653Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe graph view at `/nodes/.../projects/.../graph` renders a long flat list of amber-styled warnings, currently dominated by `duplicate document id \"AC-AGENT-001\" in \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2818c5c/docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md\"` and similar entries. Two distinct concerns:\n\n1. **Input noise** — most duplicates today come from `.claude/worktrees/\u003cagent\u003e/...` copies of the repo being indexed (tracked in `ddx-12cae4dd`). That fix will eliminate a large class of false positives but will not make the warning surface usable.\n2. **Brittle degradation** — when documents have real problems (duplicate IDs in the canonical tree, broken frontmatter, missing `depends_on` targets, `id_to_path` mismatches), the system doesn't just warn — downstream consumers (documents list, staleness, UI routing) misbehave. And there is no workflow for a user to triage, diff, or fix the offending files.\n\n## Current state of warnings\n\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte:22-30` dumps `graph.warnings: string[]` into a flat amber block. No grouping, no categorization, no links to the offending file, no way to dismiss or act.\n- Warning strings are generated in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go` (duplicate id, parse failure, required-root-missing, id_to_path mismatch/missing, cascade unknowns) as unstructured `fmt.Sprintf` output.\n- No stale-document-pruning: if a `.md` is deleted on disk, any id_to_path entry pointing at it yields a warning instead of being auto-cleaned.\n\n## Scope of this bead (deliberately not overlapping ddx-12cae4dd)\n\nAssume `.claude/worktrees` noise is gone. This bead delivers the **integrity surface** that remains valuable: a way to see, categorize, and act on real document problems.\n\n### Deliverables\n\n1. **Structured warnings** — extend `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` from `[]string` to `[]GraphIssue{kind, path, id, message, relatedPath?}` and plumb through GraphQL. Keep a `messageLines(): []string` shim for any caller still using the old shape during the grace period.\n2. **Integrity panel on /graph** — replace the flat amber block with a collapsible panel grouped by issue kind (Duplicate ID, Missing dep target, Broken id_to_path, Parse error, Required root missing). Each group shows count + expandable list; each row shows file path (clickable → document viewer), the offending ID, and a copyable message.\n3. **Summary badge** — small badge next to the page title showing total issue count, so a clean graph reads clean.\n4. **Audit GraphQL query** — `query { docGraphIssues { kind, path, id, message, relatedPath } }` usable independently of the full graph for future dashboard use. Reuses the same structured slice.\n5. **Repair affordances (minimum viable)** — for two common cases:\n   - Duplicate ID: show both file paths side-by-side and a \"copy suggested unique ID\" button (deterministic suggestion, e.g., append a short hash of the path). No auto-write.\n   - Missing dep target: show the document declaring the missing dep, and a copy-to-clipboard snippet of the frontmatter line to remove. No auto-write.\n   Auto-repair is explicitly out of scope until we trust the detection.\n6. **CLI parity** — `ddx doctor` (or `ddx docs audit`) prints the same structured issues in a terminal-friendly form, so headless users and CI can surface problems.\n\n### Out of scope\n\n- Fixing `.claude/worktrees` indexing (owned by `ddx-12cae4dd`).\n- Auto-rewrite of source files.\n- Cross-project graph integrity (today's graph is per-project).","events_attachment":"ddx-6ff597ca/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-23T04:26:29.781059658Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","feature":"FEAT-007","id":"ddx-6ff597ca","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-007","feat-008","ui","docgraph"],"notes":"**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{\"integrity\"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.\n**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional (\"--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works\"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).\n\n`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts:177` — AC 8 asks for a Playwright e2e on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target. The test directly injects `duplicate_id` and `missing_dep` objects through a mocked `/graphql` response, so it does not exercise docgraph detection, GraphQL resolver plumbing, or project fixture routing end-to-end.","owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-c7599148","status":"closed","title":"Document graph needs an integrity audit/repair surface for duplicate IDs, missing deps, and orphaned entries","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:29:46.550763124Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:523:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer looking at the personas UI, I want to understand at a glance what personas are, where they come from (library vs project), and I want to fork or create a project-local persona without leaving the UI. Library content stays read-only so I cannot accidentally mutate shared content; project content is fully editable.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Design note + policy decision recorded.** Before implementation, a one-page note in `docs/helix/02-design/` captures D1 (library vs project policy, override rules, file locations) and D2 (scoped UI additions). Implementation PR links to it.\n\n2. **Project-local persona directory.** `.ddx/personas/*.md` is a recognized location. `ddx persona --list` shows both library and project personas with a `source` tag. Override rule: a project persona with the same name as a library persona wins for that project's bindings; unit test covers this precedence.\n\n3. **GraphQL additions.** New mutations: `personaCreate(name, body, projectId) → Persona`, `personaUpdate(name, body, projectId) → Persona`, `personaDelete(name, projectId) → {ok}`. All gated on `source == \"project\"`; attempting any of them on a library persona returns a typed error. Each mutation persists to `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` under the given project.\n\n4. **CLI parity.** `ddx persona new`, `edit`, `fork`, `delete` exist and share the same underlying operations as the GraphQL mutations (no duplicated logic). Integration test asserts that a `ddx persona new` followed by a GraphQL `personas` query returns the new persona.\n\n5. **UI — explainer copy.** The personas page shows a one-sentence description of what personas are and a second sentence distinguishing library vs project sources. Playwright asserts both sentences are present.\n\n6. **UI — source badge.** Each persona row shows a `library` or `project` badge. Playwright seeds one of each and asserts correct badges.\n\n7. **UI — New / Edit / Delete for project personas.** A \"New persona\" button opens a form with name + body fields. Project-source rows have Edit and Delete affordances. Library-source rows have neither. Playwright creates a persona, edits it, verifies the edit persisted, then deletes it.\n\n8. **UI — Fork library to project.** Library rows have a \"Fork to project\" action. Clicking it prompts for a name (default: library name with `-local` suffix on collision), creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md`, and navigates to the editor. Playwright asserts the forked file exists and opens in the editor.\n\n9. **No regressions.** Existing list/show/bind flows still work. Playwright smoke of the pre-existing binding UX passes.\n\n10. **Empty project-local state.** When a project has zero project-local personas, the UI shows a subtle hint: \"No project personas yet. Fork a library persona or create a new one.\" Playwright asserts.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"69c60d902a41f3a24af908881f4ef14ba1ff3832","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:40.66472156Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`/nodes/.../projects/.../personas` is a well-crafted browser + bind UX, but it is read-only: no add, no edit, no delete, no explanation of what the reader is looking at or why they cannot change it. A user looking at it can reasonably ask \"what is this for?\" and \"why can't I change anything?\" and the UI answers neither.\n\n## Ground truth\n\n- **What a persona is.** A markdown file with frontmatter under `library/personas/*.md` defining AI personality, expertise, principles, communication style. Shipped defaults: architect, code-reviewer, implementer, specification-enforcer, test-engineer.\n- **How it's used.** Projects declare role→persona bindings in `.ddx/config.yaml` via `persona_bindings`. When an agent performs role X, the bound persona's body is injected into its system prompt.\n- **What the tool exposes today.**\n  - CLI: `ddx persona --list`, `--show \u003cname\u003e`, `--bind \u003cpersona\u003e --role \u003crole\u003e`. No create/edit/delete.\n  - GraphQL: `personaBind` mutation only (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:150`). No `personaCreate/Update/Delete` in schema.\n  - UI: list/show/bind parity with CLI.\n- **Why it evolved this way.** Personas have been treated as *library content* — authored carefully, version-controlled upstream, pulled into projects via `ddx update` and contributed back via `ddx contribute`. The tool deliberately did not become a persona editor.\n\n## The gap\n\nThe library-only model doesn't hold up for real use:\n\n1. **Project-local personas are a real need.** Teams want \"our code-reviewer, with our opinions about our codebase.\" Today they either (a) fork the persona file and push upstream (wrong — it's not generally useful), (b) never customize (bad outcomes), or (c) drop a file into some ad-hoc local path and hope it works.\n2. **The UI implies capability it doesn't have.** A list + detail view with no hint about source policy reads as \"unfinished feature\" rather than \"intentional read-only\".\n3. **Binding alone isn't enough.** Even if editing stays out of scope, users need to understand *which personas are available*, *where they come from* (library vs project-local), and *how to change that*.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo decisions (pick before implementation):\n\n### D1 — Policy: who owns persona content?\n\nRecommend: **both, with a clear boundary.**\n- Library personas: `library/personas/*.md` — read-only in UI, contribute upstream via `ddx contribute`.\n- Project-local personas: new directory `.ddx/personas/*.md` — fully editable in UI, live with the project, not in the shared library. Override semantics: a project-local persona with the same name as a library persona takes precedence for that project's bindings.\n\n### D2 — Scope of the UI\n\nMinimum viable, given D1:\n- **Explainer copy** on the personas page header: \"Personas are AI personality templates. Library personas are shared; project personas live with this project. Bind a persona to a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`.\"\n- **Source badge** on each persona row: `library` or `project`.\n- **Project-local editor.** For project personas:\n  - `New persona` button → form (name, roles/tags, body markdown). Creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` with frontmatter.\n  - `Edit` on project rows.\n  - `Delete` on project rows with confirm.\n- **Library rows stay read-only.** Action on a library row: `Fork to project` — copies the library file to `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` (with a default rename suggestion on collision) and opens the editor. No in-place edit, no delete.\n- **Bind affordance remains.** Binding UI continues to work for both sources.\n\n## What this bead does NOT decide\n\n- Multi-project persona sharing (e.g., org-level personas between library and project). File a follow-up if it becomes pressing.\n- Rich editor affordances beyond a plain markdown textarea with preview. Frontmatter can be a YAML-ish textarea for v1.\n- Syncing project personas upstream — that remains `ddx contribute` and is out of scope.\n- Any change to how personas are injected into agent prompts (the read path on the server).\n\n## CLI parity\n\nWhatever the UI gains, the CLI gains too. Add:\n- `ddx persona new \u003cname\u003e` — creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` scaffold.\n- `ddx persona edit \u003cname\u003e` — opens `$EDITOR` on the project-local persona (errors on library).\n- `ddx persona fork \u003clibrary-name\u003e [--as \u003cnew-name\u003e]` — copies library → project.\n- `ddx persona delete \u003cname\u003e` — project-local only.\n\nUI and CLI call the same underlying `cli/internal/persona` operations; no duplicate logic.","events_attachment":"ddx-9e0eff22/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-9e0eff22","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","personas","ui","design"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nThe entire diff is a single file `.ddx/executions/20260423T023822-a2d45fb0/result.json` — an execution log, not implementation code.\nZero of the required artifacts are present: no `docs/helix/02-design/` design note, no changes to `cli/internal/persona/`, no GraphQL schema or resolver additions, no SvelteKit UI changes, no Playwright tests, no CLI command additions.\nThe `result.json` claims `\"outcome\": \"task_succeeded\"` but the merged commit (`5e13d45c`) needs to be inspected — the diff provided for review shows only the evidence file, not the implementation. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:597:{"acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-loop` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T03:35:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1106936","closing_commit_sha":"8b60eb49955aa807267a5d8aa80fab5e65c8b1b6","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:18:51.589044661Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b44ca394","depends_on_id":"ddx-cf340665","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:30:57Z"}],"description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.","events_attachment":"ddx-b44ca394/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T03:39:25.89063588Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent execution failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-13T21:32:10Z","execution-eligible":true,"id":"ddx-b44ca394","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"notes":"Blocker ddx-cf340665 is closed. Ready: add type=epic filter to ReadyExecution() in cli/internal/bead/store.go + test.","owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-cf340665","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-1da6495","spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:24:48.965569064Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1154:{"acceptance":"Given a plugin package has a valid package.yaml but a declared skill directory missing SKILL.md or required frontmatter, when `ddx install --local` or registry install runs, then the command exits non-zero, writes no installed.yaml entry, and surfaces the same structural validation error that doctor would report","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:33:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"234160","closing_commit_sha":"516fc8c8a7d25dbc47e3f8ba694bb2646b2c8d67","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:20:55.587100214Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/install.go:212\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: high\nDescription: The new install paths only validate `package.yaml`, then proceed to symlink/copy skills without reusing the structural checks that doctor performs. `installLocal` calls `LoadPackageManifest` and then links skills, while `InstallPackage` does the same for tarball installs. As a result, malformed plugins still install successfully. Repro: a local plugin with a valid package.yaml and a declared skill directory containing `bad-skill/` but no `SKILL.md` installs successfully instead of failing fast.\nSuggested fix: Share one structural validator between install and doctor so install rejects missing `SKILL.md`, invalid frontmatter, and related contract violations before writing install state.","id":"hx-f7e65bcc","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: fail install when plugin skill metadata is invalid","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:33:45.197279785Z"}

The unreachable filter happens inside\n    GetProjects (state.go:335-347), which the bead path consumes before\n    delegating to beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry.\n  - The CI job \"Guard server state against test pollution\" added in\n    .github/workflows/ci.yml (commit 1f1b916c) runs the full test suite\n    under an isolated XDG_DATA_HOME, starts the server, and fails if the\n    resulting state file contains more than 20 projects or any path\n    matching the test-dir patterns.\n\nAC §5 — Backward compatibility\n  -
