.ddx/attachments/ddx-1e680a00/events.jsonl:4:{"actor":"erik","body":"verification_command passed: cd cli && go test -count=1 -run 'TestOperatorPrompt|TestRecordOperatorPrompt|TestComputeOperatorPromptAffected' ./internal/agent ./internal/server/graphql\nrationale: no_changes\nverification_command: cd cli && go test -count=1 -run 'TestOperatorPrompt|TestRecordOperatorPrompt|TestComputeOperatorPromptAffected' ./internal/agent ./internal/server/graphql\n\nreason: All three ACs are already satisfied by code merged via the prior\nsibling bead ddx-23c67ca4 (\"S15-5: Execute-loop wake-on-approve +\norigin_operator_prompt_id backlinks\", closed at commit 39ff5d16). The\ncurrent bead ddx-1e680a00 is a redundant restatement of the same S15-5\nwork under the same parent epic ddx-1d85c927. Evidence:\n\nAC1 — \"Execute loop picks up newly-ready operator-prompts\":\n  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go invokes\n    ExecuteLoopWaker.WakeProject(workingDir) on every successful\n    operatorPromptApprove and on operatorPromptSubmit auto-approve.\n  - cli/internal/server/workers.go wires WorkerManager as the waker.\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go consumes the wake signal via\n    SleepOrWake, dropping the idle-poll sleep when an approve fires.\n  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptApprove_SignalsWake,\n    TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApprove_SignalsWake,\n    TestOperatorPromptCancel_DoesNotSignalWake,\n    TestOperatorPromptApprove_NilWakerNoCrash\n    (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_wake_test.go).\n\nAC2 — \"origin_operator_prompt_id added to events / artifact frontmatter\":\n  - cli/internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks.go computes the\n    base..result diff after a successful operator-prompt execution,\n    classifies changed paths into affected beads vs artifacts, and\n    appends an `origin_operator_prompt_id` BeadEvent to every affected\n    bead plus a summary event on the originator listing every affected\n    bead and artifact path.\n  - The wiring fires from execute_bead_loop.go ~L794-810 inside the\n    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess branch, gated on\n    candidate.IssueType == bead.IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.\n  - The events form satisfies the spec wording in\n    docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021 §Bead shape: \"every artifact\n    mutated by an operator-prompt execution gets an\n    origin_operator_prompt_id event so provenance survives later edits\".\n  - Tests: TestComputeOperatorPromptAffected_DiffsBeadsAndArtifacts,\n    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_AppendsEventsOnAffectedAndOriginator,\n    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_EmptyAffectedNoEvents,\n    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_ConcurrentTargetSameBead.\n\nAC3 — \"Backlinks queryable via ddx bead show\":\n  - `ddx bead show <id> --json` calls Store.LoadEventsInline (cmd/bead.go\n    L330) which inlines events for both active and archived beads, so\n    `origin_operator_prompt_id` events are visible via the JSON shape:\n        ddx bead show <originator-or-affected-id> --json \\\n          | jq '.events[] | select(.kind == \"origin_operator_prompt_id\")'\n  - Equivalently, `ddx bead evidence list <id>` (cmd/bead.go L654-668)\n    enumerates the same events.\n\nThe verification_command above re-runs the four AC-bearing test packages\nagainst the current tree. Exit 0 ⇒ all three ACs remain satisfied, the\nbead can close as already-done.\nresult_rev=61429f5f01a91bfa793402e525ca8064186d4074\nbase_rev=61429f5f01a91bfa793402e525ca8064186d4074","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:43:52.929277629Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"already_satisfied"}
.ddx/attachments/ddx-fe1bed9d/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"erik","body":"verification_command passed: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... && lefthook run pre-commit\nrationale: verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... && lefthook run pre-commit\nreason: The requested routing is already implemented in the current tree. cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go owns the declined_needs_decomposition, push_failed, and push_conflict parking outcomes via structured Outcome/ParkingOutcome data; cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go consumes attemptOut.Parking generically and no longer classifies those statuses inline; and the acceptance tests named in the bead already exist in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_push_failed_test.go.\nresult_rev=f7852b4f2cb10c7eb7ca6ded87e38c507dabfac3\nbase_rev=f7852b4f2cb10c7eb7ca6ded87e38c507dabfac3","created_at":"2026-05-05T18:23:02.743034717Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"already_satisfied"}
.ddx/attachments/ddx-b790449b/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-b790449b — no changes this attempt.\n\n## Why this bead was not advanced in one execute-bead pass\n\nThe bead asks for `ddx work` to auto-route execution without any project\nconfig. Read literally, the eight acceptance criteria require:\n\n  AC1  Zero-config drain succeeds (project-side change + upstream relax)\n  AC2  Reword the under-specified error to point at the global config\n  AC3  Enable the triage complexity gate by default + opt-out flag\n  AC4  Auto-tier selection from bead metadata (labels/kind/scope)\n  AC5  Coarse-bead handling: decompose, OR emit a NEW `needs_triage`\n       execution status (distinct from `execution_failed`)\n  AC6  Failure recovery: retry-in-tier vs escalate-to-next-tier\n  AC7  New integration test with a temp project + minimal global config\n  AC8  Doc update presenting zero-config flow as the default\n\nEach of AC3, AC4, and AC5 is a feature-sized piece of work on its own:\n\n  - AC4 (auto-tier selection) implements a tier-inference engine over\n    bead metadata. There is no such engine today; routing currently\n    accepts operator intent or rejects with `routing under-specified`.\n    This overlaps directly with the open endpoint-first routing redesign\n    (memory: project_endpoint_routing_design — 2026-04-21: kill\n    named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy\n    model match). Implementing tier inference now without aligning with\n    that redesign would either be redone or block it.\n\n  - AC5 (`needs_triage` status) introduces a new ExecuteBeadStatus value\n    that cuts across:\n      internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go     (status enum + classify)\n      internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go       (loop handling + retry)\n      internal/bead/store.go                    (close vs unclaim policy)\n      cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go             (operator output)\n      docs/                                     (status table)\n    plus the matching test surface. This cannot share a commit with the\n    routing fix without conflating two reviewable changes.\n\n  - AC3 (triage gate default-on) is a small toggle, but the gate\n    function `w.ComplexityGate` is currently injected from outside and\n    is `nil` in the CLI path (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:506).\n    Enabling it by default requires either a default ComplexityGate\n    implementation (which is itself a dispatcher into AC4 inference) or\n    a stub that always returns `shouldClaim=true` — the latter is\n    indistinguishable from disabled and would not satisfy the AC.\n\nThe `routing under-specified` error itself is enforced in the upstream\nfizeau library at:\n\n  $GOMODCACHE/.../fizeau@v0.9.28/service_execute.go:192\n\nDDx is currently on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28. The\nshort-circuit fires when *all* of Harness, Profile, ModelRef, Model,\nProvider are empty. AC1 cannot be met purely on the DDx side without\neither (a) populating one of those fields from a sensible default\n(which is AC4) or (b) a fizeau release that lets the engine's\nResolveRoute see a \"global default_provider\" hint. Per memory\n(feedback_no_local_replace), DDx must always import a properly tagged\nagent release, so a `replace` directive is not available even\ntemporarily.\n\n## What was verified before stopping\n\n  - The under-specified error string is upstream-only — it does not\n    occur in any DDx Go file (Grep across cli/, internal/, cmd/).\n  - The flag set on `ddx agent work` already exposes --profile,\n    --provider, --harness, --model, --model-ref, --effort. The user's\n    report shows `--profile cheap` still failing, which is a separate\n    bug from the bead's main thrust: profile is set as a non-empty\n    flag default (agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, agent_cmd.go:1563) but\n    is being passed through `singleTierAttempt` which uses\n    CLIOverrides without setting Profile (agent_cmd.go:1716–1725).\n    That single-line gap is plausibly the proximate cause of the\n    `--profile cheap` regression but solving it does not satisfy the\n    broader bead.\n  - Triage gate disabled-by-default is at execute_bead_loop.go:506;\n    the gate is a function field on ExecuteBeadWorker. There is no\n    in-tree default implementation.\n\n## Suggested decomposition\n\nRecommend splitting this bead into the following children before any\nimplementation pass attempts AC1:\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Profile flag passthrough fix: ensure --profile/--provider/\n            --model-ref reach singleTierAttempt's CLIOverrides\n            (single small commit + regression test). Likely closes the\n            `--profile cheap` workaround failure.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Improve under-specified error: when global agent config has\n            providers but the request has no routing inputs, point the\n            operator at the global default_provider rather than at the\n            per-project flag list. Coordinate with fizeau release.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Tier inference engine over bead metadata\n            (kind/labels/file-touch estimate). Aligns with the\n            endpoint-first routing redesign.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  ExecuteBeadStatusNeedsTriage status + classify rules +\n            close/unclaim policy + tests.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier\n            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nIf a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to\njust AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default\nharness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,\ntreating the absence of project config as \"use global default_provider\"\nrather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.\n\nPer the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently\nexecute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a\ndispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.\nrationale: Bead ddx-b790449b — no changes this attempt.\n\n## Why this bead was not advanced in one execute-bead pass\n\nThe bead asks for `ddx work` to auto-route execution without any project\nconfig. Per memory\n(feedback_no_local_replace), DDx must always import a properly tagged\nagent release, so a `replace` directive is not available even\ntemporarily.\n\n## What was verified before stopping\n\n  - The under-specified error string is upstream-only — it does not\n    occur in any DDx Go file (Grep across cli/, internal/, cmd/).\n  - The flag set on `ddx agent work` already exposes --profile,\n    --provider, --harness, --model, --model-ref, --effort. The user's\n    report shows `--profile cheap` still failing, which is a separate\n    bug from the bead's main thrust: profile is set as a non-empty\n    flag default (agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, agent_cmd.go:1563) but\n    is being passed through `singleTierAttempt` which uses\n    CLIOverrides without setting Profile (agent_cmd.go:1716–1725).\n    That single-line gap is plausibly the proximate cause of the\n    `--profile cheap` regression but solving it does not satisfy the\n    broader bead.\n  - Triage gate disabled-by-default is at execute_bead_loop.go:506;\n    the gate is a function field on ExecuteBeadWorker. There is no\n    in-tree default implementation.\n\n## Suggested decomposition\n\nRecommend splitting this bead into the following children before any\nimplementation pass attempts AC1:\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Profile flag passthrough fix: ensure --profile/--provider/\n            --model-ref reach singleTierAttempt's CLIOverrides\n            (single small commit + regression test). Likely closes the\n            `--profile cheap` workaround failure.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Improve under-specified error: when global agent config has\n            providers but the request has no routing inputs, point the\n            operator at the global default_provider rather than at the\n            per-project flag list. Coordinate with fizeau release.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Tier inference engine over bead metadata\n            (kind/labels/file-touch estimate). Aligns with the\n            endpoint-first routing redesign.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  ExecuteBeadStatusNeedsTriage status + classify rules +\n            close/unclaim policy + tests.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier\n            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).\n\n  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nIf a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to\njust AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default\nharness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,\ntreating the absence of project config as \"use global default_provider\"\nrather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.\n\nPer the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently\nexecute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a\ndispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.\nresult_rev=68f5c39d11bb6e4e7e9a9327691d9baf3420b3cc\nbase_rev=68f5c39d11bb6e4e7e9a9327691d9baf3420b3cc","created_at":"2026-05-01T20:53:16.329540344Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"already_satisfied"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-7e9cc0cc/events.jsonl:4:{"actor":"erik","body":"verification_command passed: cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/\nrationale: All acceptance criteria already satisfied at base-rev e82a9bc6.\n\nThis bead (ddx-7e9cc0cc) duplicates ddx-1bbd1096 (\"B14.2: cli/internal/federation\npackage - state types + persistence + schema_version\"), which was closed and\nlanded in commit 0dd3cead \"feat(federation): add internal/federation state\ntypes + persistence [ddx-1bbd1096]\" — an ancestor of base-rev.\n\nAC1 — internal/federation/ package with state types: SATISFIED.\n  cli/internal/federation/state.go defines:\n    - FederationRegistry { SchemaVersion, Spokes }\n    - SpokeRecord { NodeID, Name, URL, DDxVersion, SchemaVersion, Capabilities,\n      RegisteredAt, LastHeartbeat, Status } — keyed by stable NodeID\n    - SpokeStatus enum: registered/active/stale/offline/degraded (+ Valid())\n    - NewRegistry, FindSpoke, UpsertSpoke (NodeID-keyed insert/replace),\n      RemoveSpoke, SetStatus, ReconcileLiveness\n  Heartbeat data lives on SpokeRecord.LastHeartbeat (time.Time); registration\n  version on SpokeRecord.SchemaVersion / DDxVersion plus the registry-level\n  CurrentSchemaVersion constant. The bead's named types (Spoke, Heartbeat,\n  RegistrationVersion) map onto these existing fields; no new wrapper types\n  are needed and adding them would diverge from the call sites already wired\n  in fanout.go, spoke.go, handshake.go.\n\nAC2 — persistence layer with atomic writes: SATISFIED.\n  cli/internal/federation/persistence.go:\n    - DefaultStatePath honors XDG_DATA_HOME, default ~/.local/share/ddx/\n      federation-state.json (the \"or similar\" path slot in the bead).\n    - SaveStateTo: marshal -> tmpfile in target dir -> fsync -> chmod 0600 ->\n      atomic rename. writeMu serializes intra-process writers.\n    - LoadStateFrom: missing-file -> fresh registry; malformed JSON ->\n      quarantine to \"<path>.corrupt-<ts>\" and return fresh registry;\n      pre-versioned files default to CurrentSchemaVersion.\n\nAC3 — tests: SATISFIED.\n  cli/internal/federation/state_test.go (313 lines) covers round-trip,\n  missing-file, malformed-file recovery, status transitions, NodeID upsert\n  semantics. `go test ./internal/federation/` passes (verified at this rev).\n\nNo code changes were necessary or appropriate; implementing again would\nduplicate or churn working, integrated code that downstream beads\n(ddx-ab7f6184, ddx-238880c4, ddx-89bc2db4) already depend on.\n\nverification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/\nresult_rev=e82a9bc68ce2e32f70e8f81769c4c09c158a049d\nbase_rev=e82a9bc68ce2e32f70e8f81769c4c09c158a049d","created_at":"2026-05-03T16:29:45.915499383Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"already_satisfied"}

.ddx/executions/20260627T205852-aa732710/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
  During the 2026-06-11 dogfood drill in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, `ddx work focus` reported "24 bead(s) ready and no active workers detected; consider running: ddx work" while `ddx work --once` immediately exited with operator attention because the project root had uncommitted tracked frontend/e2e changes. The stewardship surface therefore recommends an action the worker preflight already knows cannot proceed.

ROOT CAUSE
  `cli/cmd/work_focus.go:136-165` builds the ready summary and worker recommendation from `store.ReadyExecution()` plus active worker count only. `cli/cmd/work_focus.go:198-215` then recommends `ddx work` whenever readyCount is nonzero and no active worker is observed. The dirty-project-root guard lives separately in the worker loop at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2096-2130`, so `ddx work focus` does not consult or mirror the preflight blocker before recommending a worker start.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In `cli/cmd/work_focus.go`, add a project-root dirty preflight check to the focus report path when projectRoot is available.
  - Surface dirty tracked paths in `unknowns` or a new operator-facing focus field, and make `WorkerRecommendation` say to resolve/commit/stash dirty paths before running `ddx work`.
  - Keep the current ready-depth reporting intact so operators still see queue pressure.
  - Add focused tests in `cli/cmd/work_focus_test.go` for dirty root suppression and clean root behavior.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not weaken the worker dirty-root guard in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.
  - Do not auto-stash, auto-commit, or mutate the operator checkout from `ddx work focus`; it must remain read-only.
  - Do not change queue priority/rank semantics.

PARENT + DEPS
  No parent. No deps.

.ddx/executions/20260630T113734-dc865ab5/prompt.md:8:# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:server, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-002, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049 (axon backend prototype umbrella).
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (interface refinement, which adds `BeadReader` and the LifecycleSubscriber ctx-aware signature).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

.ddx/executions/20260630T125813-4114eb07/prompt.md:8:# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 archive/interchange methods (`Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile`). Per TD-027 §6 archive belongs on `BeadArchive` interface; interchange on `BeadInterchangeReader`/`BeadInterchangeWriter`. All take ctx per AC #1. These are signature-only changes — no helper migration needed; the methods stay as direct storage primitives.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.
3. `ddx bead archive`, `ddx bead export`, `ddx bead import` CLI commands continue to work end-to-end. Test: `TestBeadArchiveCommand_RoundTrip`, `TestBeadExportImport_RoundTripsAllFields`.
4. Schema round-trip preserved (per TD-027 §20 — bd/br interchange compatibility). Test: `TestBead_ExportImportRoundTrip_PreservesExtras`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:archive, area:interchange, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.

The codex that the guard claims to have \"terminated\" is alive. When I manually `kill 1229443`, the worker immediately respawns a fresh codex (1416758) within seconds — same non-route state. Worker itself is in `futex_wait_queue` in kernel wait.\n\nLog shows `worker.heartbeat` every ~30s with `phase=running` but no actual work events for 1h+. Zero bead outcome events (no ✓/✗/no_changes/timeout/etc.).\n\n# ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS\nTwo candidate bugs:\n\n1. **Running-phase guard doesn't actually kill**: some code path emits the `terminated by running-phase guard` diagnostic string without calling kill/wait on the non-route pid. So the message is aspirational, not reflective.\n\n2. **Claude harness dispatch died but the worker didn't detect it**: the ExecuteBeadLoop harness=claude route's process died (SIGKILL, panic, EOF on stream) without emitting a final event, so the worker's outcome detection never fires. Then something (readiness probe, review dispatch, model discovery?) keeps re-invoking codex as a background helper, hitting the guard, spinning.\n\nGrep entry points:\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1483` (Stop/watchdog reap)\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (main execute loop)\n- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process*.go` (reaper)\n- Any code emitting the exact string `non-route provider ... terminated by running-phase guard` — that's the diagnostic source\n\n# PROPOSED FIX\n1. Locate the emission site of the `\"non-route provider %s terminated by running-phase guard\"` diagnostic. Verify it actually calls `syscall.Kill(pid, SIGTERM)` (or SIGKILL after grace) — NOT just labels the child. Add a wait-with-timeout to prove termination.\n2. Add a watchdog: if `provider_children[].non_route=true` for a child that has been alive \u003eN seconds AND the child's pid still exists AND no successor process replaced it via a legitimate route, kill it hard (SIGKILL + wait) and log at level ERROR.\n3. When the claim's route harness (claude/opus) subprocess exits without emitting a final event, the worker MUST synthesize a terminal outcome (execution_failed or transport-classified) instead of remaining `phase=running` forever. Currently it appears to just sit in futex_wait indefinitely.\n\n# NON-SCOPE\n- The specific test-harness deadlock inside ddx-68b48c9e itself (that's a separate story if it exists).\n- Server-managed workers (ddx-9d1af129 epic) — this bug affects both server-managed and standalone `ddx work`.\n\n# IMPACT\nWithout this fix, any transient claude subprocess death mid-attempt puts a worker into a permanent stuck state with 100% CPU codex spawn churn. Test `TestWork_ClaudeSubprocessDeathSynthesizesTerminalOutcome` proves: if the claude harness subprocess exits without emitting a final event, the worker synthesizes an execution_failed (or transport:*) outcome within 30s AND writes a terminal worker.heartbeat with phase!=running.\n3. `ddx work --watch` no longer enters the observed pattern (`phase=running`, `harness=claude`, no claude child, non_route codex alive, heartbeats forever, futex_wait) — verified by a scripted end-to-end test that kills the claude subprocess mid-attempt and observes the worker exiting the attempt within 60s.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NonRoute|Reaped|SubprocessDeath|StayAlive' -count=1` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.",
.ddx/executions/20260702T142657-749851dd/prompt.md:8:# PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` gets into an unrecoverable spawn-loop where the worker keeps launching codex subprocesses that its own status.json labels as "non-route provider ... terminated by running-phase guard" — but the guard does NOT terminate them. Result: no bead progress, stale claude/opus attempt state, infinite codex churn. Observed live 2026-07-02: worker pid 3893557 on bead ddx-68b48c9e, attempt 20260702T105934-18be73ef.

# EVIDENCE (captured)
Worker `status.json` reports:
- `harness: claude`, `model: opus`, `phase: running`
- `provider_children: [{pid: 1229443, provider: codex, non_route: true, diagnostic: "non-route provider codex terminated by running-phase guard (active route claude)"}]`

BUT `ps --ppid 3893557` shows only:
- `git` (age 14:41)
- `codex` (age 14:17, the "non-route" one)
- NO claude process

The claude/opus process that the status says is "running" is gone. The codex that the guard claims to have "terminated" is alive. When I manually `kill 1229443`, the worker immediately respawns a fresh codex (1416758) within seconds — same non-route state. Worker itself is in `futex_wait_queue` in kernel wait.

Log shows `worker.heartbeat` every ~30s with `phase=running` but no actual work events for 1h+. Zero bead outcome events (no ✓/✗/no_changes/timeout/etc.).

# ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
Two candidate bugs:

1. **Running-phase guard doesn't actually kill**: some code path emits the `terminated by running-phase guard` diagnostic string without calling kill/wait on the non-route pid. So the message is aspirational, not reflective.

2. **Claude harness dispatch died but the worker didn't detect it**: the ExecuteBeadLoop harness=claude route's process died (SIGKILL, panic, EOF on stream) without emitting a final event, so the worker's outcome detection never fires. Then something (readiness probe, review dispatch, model discovery?) keeps re-invoking codex as a background helper, hitting the guard, spinning.

Grep entry points:
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1483` (Stop/watchdog reap)
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (main execute loop)
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process*.go` (reaper)
- Any code emitting the exact string `non-route provider ... terminated by running-phase guard` — that's the diagnostic source

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Locate the emission site of the `"non-route provider %s terminated by running-phase guard"` diagnostic. Verify it actually calls `syscall.Kill(pid, SIGTERM)` (or SIGKILL after grace) — NOT just labels the child. Add a wait-with-timeout to prove termination.
2. Add a watchdog: if `provider_children[].non_route=true` for a child that has been alive >N seconds AND the child's pid still exists AND no successor process replaced it via a legitimate route, kill it hard (SIGKILL + wait) and log at level ERROR.
3. When the claim's route harness (claude/opus) subprocess exits without emitting a final event, the worker MUST synthesize a terminal outcome (execution_failed or transport-classified) instead of remaining `phase=running` forever. Currently it appears to just sit in futex_wait indefinitely.

# NON-SCOPE
- The specific test-harness deadlock inside ddx-68b48c9e itself (that's a separate story if it exists).
- Server-managed workers (ddx-9d1af129 epic) — this bug affects both server-managed and standalone `ddx work`.

# IMPACT
Without this fix, any transient claude subprocess death mid-attempt puts a worker into a permanent stuck state with 100% CPU codex spawn churn. Queue drain stalls. On my machine this required manual `kill 1229443` + kill worker + restart to unstick.
.ddx/executions/20260702T142657-749851dd/prompt.md:11:1. Test `TestWork_NonRouteProviderIsActuallyReaped` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go` (or a new file) proves: given a running worker with harness=claude, spawning a codex child then labeling it non_route, the child process is killed (via syscall.Kill + wait) within 10s and does NOT respawn.
2. Test `TestWork_ClaudeSubprocessDeathSynthesizesTerminalOutcome` proves: if the claude harness subprocess exits without emitting a final event, the worker synthesizes an execution_failed (or transport:*) outcome within 30s AND writes a terminal worker.heartbeat with phase!=running.
3. `ddx work --watch` no longer enters the observed pattern (`phase=running`, `harness=claude`, no claude child, non_route codex alive, heartbeats forever, futex_wait) — verified by a scripted end-to-end test that kills the claude subprocess mid-attempt and observes the worker exiting the attempt within 60s.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NonRoute|Reaped|SubprocessDeath|StayAlive' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS

.ddx/executions/20260629T093128-3ac391c3/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Server-managed drain workers are still started as in-process goroutines, so their WorkerRecord usually has PID zero and no PGID. The server cannot reliably target the ddx work process tree that later launches claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:123-127 records only PID and has no persisted PGID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as managing in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:461-462 starts drainProgress and runWorker goroutines instead of a server-owned OS process. cli/internal/server/workers.go:414-436 writes the initial WorkerRecord before any managed worker process boundary exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a server-managed subprocess launch path for StartExecuteLoop in cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-464. Persist the ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as today, then start the current ddx binary as ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> or an equivalent hidden internal entry point in its own process group. Record nonzero PID and PGID on WorkerRecord and status.json. Keep the existing in-process path only where tests explicitly inject it, and do not make externally reported workers look server-owned.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP

PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.

- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` adds self-contained Niflheim evidence cases for both observed details: `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`; it asserts the selected action is reconcile, retry-land, preserve-evidence, or operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run.
- `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower` proves land/ref/index/evidence-staging outcomes do not raise requested min power/profile and do not request stronger model escalation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

Observed in production on 2026-06-27: ddx worker pid 3577575 emitted '▶ ddx-d3abb526' (claim) immediately followed by '✗ ddx-d3abb526 → recoverable: bead: not found: ddx-d3abb526'. ddx-d3abb526 ("Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns: part 3", task, status=open) was visible in 'ddx bead ready' both before and after the failure event. The bead was not deleted, archived, or rotated. Indicates a race in the worker between the claim path and the subsequent bead-load path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- Worker watch loop dispatches 'ddx try <id>' after successful claim. The try path re-loads the bead from .ddx/beads.jsonl by id. If the in-memory index is stale w.r.t. concurrent tracker writes (concurrent 'ddx bead update'/'close' from another worker or the operator), the re-load can miss a bead the claim step just touched.
- Specifically inspect:
  - cli/cmd/try.go: how the bead is fetched after the worker hands off the claimed id
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: bead.Load by id and its error classification
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go (or equivalent): index refresh semantics

PROPOSED FIX
- After a successful claim, thread the in-memory bead handle from the claim step into the try-execution path; do not re-load by id from a possibly-stale index.
- If re-load is structurally required, on 'bead: not found' force an index refresh and retry once before declaring 'recoverable'.

OUT OF SCOPE
- Do not change the recoverable retry policy itself.
- Do not change the queue scoring/ordering.

AC
1. Add unit test Test_WorkClaim_NotFoundRace in cli/cmd/work_test.go (or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go) that simulates a worker claim followed by a stale-index lookup, and asserts the worker retries with refreshed index rather than failing.
2. Add a regression test that runs the watch loop against a tracker under concurrent 'ddx bead update' churn and asserts no 'bead: not found: <id>' failures for ids present in the tracker at failure time.
3. The exact case observed today (ddx-d3abb526 visible in ready, claimed, then load-failed) cannot reproduce.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes

- `cli/cmd/install.go:181` is the install-directory decision point that must choose `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/` for global installs or `ddxroot.Path()` for project installs.
- `cli/cmd/install.go:270` and `cli/cmd/install.go:308` define `ddx plugin list/show`; they need the same `--global` state selection as install.
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90` defines the global DDx state root required by ddx-06cbaa90.

PROPOSED FIX
Add or verify `--global` on `ddx install`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx plugin show`. Global installs write plugin content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`, record global installed state, and create agent-tier links under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`. Plain installs write to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/`; in in-tree mode that resolves to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, and in convention mode to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/`. Plain installs create project-tier skill links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`, with convention-mode links pointing into the XDG project plugin tree.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a.
- Depends on ddx-06cbaa90: install paths route through both `ddxroot.Path()` and `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/`.
- ADR-027 skill install topology or equivalent must stay consistent with this behavior.

PARENT
- ddx-602ae31a

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely; global and per-project modes coexist.
- Migrating `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths if any exist.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex-facing `.agents/skills`; add others only when those harnesses define their skill paths.

Operator Stop must fully clean a server-managed worker, including claude, codex, shell, and sleep descendants, without killing unrelated processes or duplicating bead transitions when Stop is called more than once.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1227 has an idempotence guard for in-memory handles, but the cleanup path still depends on a single recorded PID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 calls terminateProcessGroup only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1288-1289 then cancels the goroutine, which cannot guarantee cleanup of harness descendants when the server-owned worker lacks a process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:22-32 documents that callers must register only processes spawned in their own process group.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce or reuse one cleanup helper that terminates only server-owned managed worker process groups by PGID when available, falling back to registered child process groups for any transitional in-process harness path. Wire WorkerManager.Stop through that helper after the existing bead.stopped event and Unclaim call. The helper must send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, send SIGKILL to remaining descendants, report what it killed, and make repeated Stop calls no-ops for bead transitions and process cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.

- Adjacent test ~line 726 (TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt) uses the correct pattern: 30s outer timeout, worker.Run in a goroutine, require.Eventually polling for bead closure, then cancel(). See the comment at lines 772-774 explaining why 750ms was too tight.

PROPOSED FIX
Rewrite TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt to match the surrounding require.Eventually pattern:
1. Outer ctx timeout: 30*time.Second.

5. Assertions become: outcome.err is one of context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded (use require.True with errors.Is check), Attempts=1, Successes=1, Failures=0, OperatorAttention nil, no "loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved" event, no refs/ddx/pre-dispatch references.

.ddx/executions/20260703T232515-95728b40/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

.ddx/executions/20260629T090840-d64351ac/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/executions/20260703T122452-6563ee59/prompt.md:15:PROBLEM
Dogfood generated `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` large enough to trigger the pre-commit large-file guard at about 5 MB. DDx-managed lock metrics are useful audit data, but unbounded growth turns routine worker execution into a commit blocker and leaves dirty runtime state after attempts.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go defines lock caps, and DDx writes lock metrics under `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl`, but there is no bounded retention/rotation policy comparable to the execution cleanup guarantees in docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8. The durable audit path work in ddx-cd99987d covers force-staging managed paths, but it does not cap or rotate oversized lock metric files.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add bounded retention or rotation for `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` with an explicit size/age policy.
- Keep recent lock violation evidence durable while preventing normal lock telemetry from exceeding repository large-file gates.
- Ensure worker shutdown/cleanup can leave the worktree clean or with only intentionally staged DDx-managed audit paths.
- Document the retention policy near the lock metrics implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not delete lock-violation evidence needed for debugging. Do not disable the large-file pre-commit guard. Do not silently ignore lock metrics write failures.

.ddx/executions/20260628T073443-5c99b59b/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
DDx cleanup dry-run can remove or report stale execution files, but it cannot tell an operator that a stale DDx-owned descendant process is still alive under a current-project execution worktree. This leaves orphan shells, cargo test or benchmark processes, and tiny long-lived test servers invisible during recovery.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` defines `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with file/resource counts only, so process findings, process-group counts, stale reasons, and `would_kill` status cannot be represented.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` cleans temp dirs, registered worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, agent logs, worker dirs, and candidate refs, but has no process census tied to DDx attempt worktrees.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` covers only PID-1 children whose command basename looks like a top-level harness and whose owner PID is gone; it does not report non-harness descendants left under stale execution worktrees.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` already gives normal harness children process-group and parent-death behavior, but cleanup has no later operator-visible census for descendants that survive after the harness root exits.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an internal, injectable stale attempt process scanner to execution cleanup. On Linux, collect `/proc` facts equivalent to the existing process-scanning style: pid, ppid, process group when available, command or truncated command, cwd, start time when available, and enough project ownership context to tie the process to the current project's DDx execution root or a known attempt worktree. Extend `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with process findings, counts, unavailable status, blocked warnings, stale reason, and `would_kill=true` for dry-run candidates. Dry-run must report candidates and must not call the killer. Add an unsupported-platform no-op/report-unavailable implementation. Detect non-harness descendants by cwd/worktree/liveness ownership evidence, not by broad command-name matching.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- `spec:FEAT-010`
- `spec:SD-025`

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.

.ddx/executions/20260627T230343-09464730/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
  During the 2026-06-11 dogfood drill, running `ddx work --once` against a dirty project root wrote `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json`. `git status --short` then showed the guard file as untracked. A worker preflight that exits before claiming work should not leave new, unignored repository noise for the operator to triage.

ROOT CAUSE
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:958` defines the guard state filename as `dirty-root-guard.json`. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1256-1266` writes that state under the project's `.ddx` directory, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1329-1390` updates it during dirty-root preflight. `.gitignore:420-435` ignores other DDx runtime scratch paths such as `.ddx/run-state.json`, `.ddx/run-state/`, `.ddx/workers/`, and `.ddx/*.tmp`, but does not ignore `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json`.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Treat dirty-root guard state as DDx runtime scratch, not tracked project state.
  - Add `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json` to the repository `.gitignore` and to the DDx init/default ignore entries in `cli/cmd/init.go` so new projects do not see the file as untracked.
  - Add or update tests in `cli/cmd/init_test.go` to prove generated gitignore content ignores `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json` while still preserving intentionally tracked execution evidence exceptions.
  - Keep the existing dirty-root escalation behavior and state file format unchanged unless a smaller existing runtime-state helper makes the ignore behavior cleaner.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not remove the dirty-root cooldown/escalation state machine.
  - Do not make `ddx work` proceed on a dirty project root.
  - Do not broaden ignores to all of `.ddx/`, because beads, attachments, metrics, and selected execution evidence remain intentionally tracked.

PARENT + DEPS
  No parent. No deps.

.ddx/executions/20260630T002703-95522d97/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/executions/20260630T080154-255d9ae0/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Stop currently performs ad hoc cleanup inside WorkerManager.Stop, releasing the claimed bead and then killing only the recorded pid when pid > 0. That path does not prove that a managed worker tree with fake claude/codex descendants is fully terminated, and the cleanup logic is not centralized for reuse by other lifecycle paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1312 contains Stop, including the current pid-gated terminateProcessGroup call.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234-1286 computes pid and only escalates when pid > 0, so cleanup is limited to the recorded leader process.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1298-1312 finalizes the worker after the ad hoc kill path, which makes idempotence depend on repeated copies of the same logic rather than a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a shared managed-worker tree cleanup helper that sends SIGTERM to the managed process group, waits WatchdogKillGrace, then SIGKILLs any remaining descendants.
- Make Stop call that helper for server-managed workers before terminalizing the record.
- Preserve the bead.stopped event, claim release, and terminal status writes so repeated Stop calls remain idempotent.
- Add fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children so the test can prove the full descendant tree is gone after Stop.
- Add a second Stop call assertion so the test proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change watchdog reaping in this bead.
- Do not implement server shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or interactive sessions.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent" passes.
4. make install is run after the code change lands.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

PROBLEM

Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:

.ddx/executions/20260703T202556-98826e95/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

.ddx/executions/20260630T062602-0488ecd7/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
.ddx/executions/20260629T111036-3759f345/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Observed on 2026-06-29 in this repo while draining the queue. Worker pid 4014720 hit 4 consecutive 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes within ~2h on different beads (ddx-9aa2151d, ddx-95d80f7e, ddx-1c865bdb, ddx-aef568e7) without any successful merge. Load average was 38-52 throughout (8-core host). After each transport failure the worker advances to the next ready bead and the cycle repeats; closed counter unchanged across the period.

Specific log signature:
  do response complete in <N>m
  tracker_lock section=durable_audit wait=1ms hold=<seconds>
  ✗ ddx-<id> → transport: exit status 1

Some attempts had very short responses (1m37s) suggesting codex died early; others completed long (32m+) then died at durable_audit. Common factor: the provider subprocess exits status 1, which DDx classifies as 'transport' (not tests_red, not no_changes, not timeout).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The outcome classifier maps any non-zero provider exit (codex/claude/etc.) to 'transport: exit status 1' regardless of cause (OOM, kill, network error mid-stream). Inspect cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around the outcome-mapping path for the codex/provider exit code (search 'transport: exit status'). The classifier does not distinguish 'load-killed' from 'genuine bug' from 'provider API error'.
- Under high system load (load avg > ~3-5x CPU count), the provider subprocess gets starved during the durable_audit / land phase (which is git+disk heavy), bumps into kernel OOM-killer or hits its own internal heartbeat/timeout, and exits non-zero. DDx then walks the queue trying the next bead which also dies, burning context+tokens with zero forward progress.
- There is no backoff/cooldown when transport failures cluster (e.g., 3+ in N minutes).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add load-pressure detection: before each claim, sample /proc/loadavg and compare to runtime.NumCPU(); if 5-min loadavg > k * cores (k=2-3 reasonable), emit a structured backoff observation and sleep proportional to overload before claiming.
2. Add transport-failure cooldown: when N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes occur within the last M minutes, the worker should pause for a longer interval (e.g., 5-15 min) before next claim instead of immediately consuming another bead.
3. Capture and surface the provider subprocess's exit reason where possible: check WaitStatus for signal-killed (SIGKILL → OOM-likely, SIGTERM → graceful), and distinguish in the outcome string (e.g., 'transport: provider killed by SIGKILL (likely OOM)').

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (outcome classifier + claim-loop pacing)
- cli/cmd/work.go (worker pacing/backoff)
- cli/internal/workerstatus/ (if load sampling needs a place)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not change the queue ordering.
- Do not change the durable_audit commit flow itself.
- Do not implement a general-purpose host load manager.

AC
1. New helper LoadPressureSnapshot() in cli/internal/workerstatus or cli/internal/agent returning normalized loadavg/cores; unit test covers >threshold and <threshold paths.
2. Worker pre-claim path checks the snapshot; when pressure exceeds k (default 2.5), logs a structured 'load_pressure_backoff' observation and sleeps before claim. Unit test asserts the path is invoked with k configurable.
3. Worker tracks last N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes by wall-clock timestamp; when 3 occur within 10m, the next claim is delayed by an extended cooldown (>=5m). Unit test simulates 3 failures within window and asserts the delay.
4. Outcome classifier distinguishes signal-killed (SIGKILL/SIGTERM) provider exits in the outcome string. Unit test asserts the distinct outcomes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/workerstatus/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadRe
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
