These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

HELIX is now available as a marketplace plugin, so the plugin model should feel like an npx dependency: projects record intent, payloads live outside the repo, and local agent surfaces are generated adapter state. Marketplace plugin installs already do this, but the built-in ddx skill remains an exception: ddx init / ddx plugin sync copy the full embedded ddx skill tree into .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx as real files. Those paths are ignored by ddx init today, but the behavior still normalizes generated asset trees in every project instead of a cache-backed adapter surface.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/skills/install.go:20-21 documents skills.Install as copying skills into .agents/skills and .claude/skills as real files and never creating symlinks.
- cli/cmd/builtin_plugin_sync.go:16-26 calls skills.Install over the baked-in default plugin FS, so the built-in ddx adapter is copied from embedded files instead of resolved through the same cache/shim path as registry plugins.
- cli/cmd/plugin_materialization_test.go:71-90 explicitly blesses built-in ddx adapters as real-file directories, not symlinked/cache-backed generated adapters.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md:27-33 and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:278-285 define the npx-like model and generated shims/links, but they do not spell out whether the built-in ddx adapter should also use an XDG cache materialization layer.

PROPOSED FIX
Materialize the embedded default ddx plugin payload into the XDG plugin cache on demand, then expose .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx as generated adapter shims/links to the cached skills/ddx directory. Keep the baked-in embedded FS as the offline source of truth and fallback when the cache is missing. Preserve the existing guarantee that plain ddx init does not create .ddx/plugins/ddx or require network access.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make HELIX built into DDx.
- Do not change marketplace plugin install semantics that already write plugins.lock.yaml and XDG cache payloads.
- Do not require project repositories to commit marketplace plugin payload trees or generated skill adapter directories.
- Do not remove local developer overlays; ddx plugin install <name> --local <path> remains a symlink-to-checkout mode.
- Do not reintroduce global/home plugin state.","id":"ddx-744f196d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:skills","area:cli","kind:feature","kind:design","phase:build","spec:FEAT-015","adr:027","marketplace","initiative:zero-footprint"],"parent":"ddx-602ae31a","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-015","status":"closed","title":"plugin: make built-in DDx adapters cache-backed","updated_at":"2026-06-16T06:28:04.196723168Z"}

Without `--global`, `ddx install`/`ddx update` must target the active project install location, which differs by mode: in-tree mode (a `<project>/.ddx/` exists) writes under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`; convention mode (no `.ddx/`) writes under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/`. In both modes the project-scoped agent skill dirs (`<project>/.claude/skills/` and `<project>/.agents/skills/`) must link into whichever plugins/ path the mode resolves to.

ROOT CAUSE
- For global=false, `resolveInstallDirs` (cli/cmd/install.go:184) returns installRoot=`ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs under `f.WorkingDir/.agents/skills` and `f.WorkingDir/.claude/skills` (install.go:195-197). `ddxroot.Path()` is what selects the in-tree vs convention plugins root, so the install root automatically follows the mode.
- `ddx update` (no flag) must update the project install in whichever mode is active; the non-global path in update.go (dispatched when `opts.Global` is false, update.go:66) operates on the project working dir's resolved root.
- Project installs auto-commit trackable project changes (install.go:174-176) — global installs do not.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx install <name>` (no --global) in in-tree mode installs to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`.
2. Verify `ddx install <name>` (no --global) in convention mode installs to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and the project-tier links point into that XDG plugins/ path.
3. Verify `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active and does not touch the global tree.
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of mode.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface (sibling child).
- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project install root is computed by `ddxroot.Path()`, which encodes the in-tree vs convention decision.","events_attachment":"ddx-747f1b35/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-747f1b35","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","initiative:zero-footprint","kind:feature","phase:3","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"queue-rank":1540,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b48acb35","status":"closed","title":"install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode + project-tier skill links","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:33.797287622Z"}

Desc:     PROBLEM
`ddx-359e812b` is broader than the remaining gap. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` already isolates TC-018.4 and TC-019.4 for hub-to-spoke bead and document writes, while the existing Playwright coverage in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256` already covers the mocked forwarded paths. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
FEAT-026 routes `beadCreate`, `beadUpdate`, `beadApprove`, `beadBlock`, `beadCancel`, `beadReopen`, and `documentWrite` only to the owner node and never broadcasts (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`). The stale bead mixed that live owner-targeted write persistence work with already-covered UI smoke and the separate ts-net guard.

PROPOSED FIX
- Track only the live owner-targeted write persistence gap: bead create/edit and `documentWrite` forwarding, offline-spoke refusal, and idempotent request IDs.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate bead if it still needs work.
- Guard the narrowed scope with `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Do not expand this bead back into federation read smoke or mocked Playwright smoke coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths into this bead.

In-scope files:
- `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts`

Out-of-scope (separate bead):
- ts-net guard work if it remains open

erik     1886099  336808  5 20:59 ?        00:00:09 /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-19f9c9c0-20260704T005836-b70508e6 -m gpt-5.4-mini <execute-bead>   <instructions> You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-19f9c9c0">     <title>federation: narrow the bead/document write follow-up bead</title>     <description> PROBLEM

data.text: The Playwright failures line up with the exact bead/spec-forwarding assertions this record is referencing. I’m waiting for the final report so I can tell whether there’s any harness noise left or if these are the same functional gaps the stale bead was already tracking.

The current repo state is not green enough to justify a commit: the exact Playwright specs still fail on existing UI assertions, and the exact Go package command only passes in `-short` because of the perf fixture. I’m recording that as an explicit retryable blocker in the execution bundle so the orchestrator has durable evidence instead of a silent stop.

The bead record is narrowed locally, and I wrote the retryable blocker note here: [`.ddx/executions/20260704T005836-b70508e6/no_changes_rationale.txt`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-19f9c9c0-20260704T005836-b70508e6/.ddx/executions/20260704T005836-b70508e6/no_changes_rationale.txt).

What I verified:
- `ddx bead update ddx-359e812b` now points at live owner-targeted bead/document write persistence, with the read smoke explicitly called out as already covered.
- `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` passes.
- `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` still fails on existing UI assertions:
  - missing `projectId` forwarding
  - missing edit/approve buttons
  - missing stale-write errors
  - missing graph refresh affordances
- The exact non-`-short` Go command fails on the `ProviderTrendPerfFixture` perf assertion under this host’s current load.

I did not commit, because the acceptance gates are not green.
